data.narrative:
Classiq is a Series C quantum computing software company that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation framework draws from a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and 8 private entities including Infleqtion, Quantinuum, and others operating across quantum hardware, software, and hybrid solutions.

A stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $95M capital raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $162M. A peer-median approach was not applicable given insufficient narrow comparables within quantum computing at matching stage and valuation tier; a median derived from the broader 7-company sector set risked being unanchored. The stage-progression method therefore serves as the primary triangulation point.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $137M (low) to $186M (high), centered on $162M, reflecting execution risk and the early-stage liquidity discount typical of Series C quantum software businesses. Key uncertainties include path to revenue and competitive positioning relative to hardware vendors, neither of which can be evaluated from valuation data alone. The single-method foundation also means this estimate carries higher sensitivity to stage-step assumptions than a multi-method consensus would.
